protocol_definition.name: Single Agent
protocol_definition.description: A single LLM completes the task without inter-agent communication.

messages.content: You are a careful single-agent baseline. Complete the task directly using the required format and visible task information.

Complete this benchmark task as a single-agent baseline.

Task ID: SP-02

Category: Strategic Planning

Difficulty: Medium

Tool Requirement: Prohibited

Prompt:
Create a 12-week strategic launch plan for a pilot AI course-QA assistant at a university department.

Scenario: The pilot will support two undergraduate courses with about 300 total students. The team has one part-time faculty sponsor, two student developers, one teaching assistant, and a $5,000 pilot budget. The assistant can answer questions only from instructor-approved course materials and must include safeguards for hallucination, privacy, academic-integrity concerns, and escalation to human staff.

The plan should define phases, milestones, owners, success metrics, risks, and decision gates for whether to expand the pilot after 12 weeks.

Do not invent university policies or completed pilot results. Clearly label assumptions, keep the plan within the $5,000 budget, and restrict assistant answers to instructor-approved materials.

Required Output Format:
Use the following format:
1. Executive summary
2. Goals and non-goals
3. Stakeholder map
4. 12-week roadmap table with phases, tasks, owners, and deliverables
5. Resource and budget allocation
6. Risk register with mitigations
7. Success metrics and data collection plan
8. Governance and escalation process
9. Go / no-go decision criteria for expansion
10. Final recommendation


Follow the required output format exactly. Do not use external tools if the task prohibits them.

final_output:
1. Executive summary
This plan outlines a 12-week pilot launch for an AI course-QA assistant at a university department, supporting two undergraduate courses (~300 students). With a small team (one part-time faculty sponsor, two student developers, one TA) and a $5,000 budget, the assistant will answer questions only from instructor-approved materials, with safeguards against hallucination, privacy breaches, academic-integrity issues, and escalation to human staff. The pilot is structured in four phases: foundation, development, testing, and live deployment with evaluation. Success metrics focus on accuracy, usage, user satisfaction, and cost. A go/no-go decision at week 12 determines expansion.

2. Goals and non-goals
- Goals:
  - Deploy a functional AI assistant that answers course-related questions using only instructor-approved materials.
  - Achieve ≥80% answer accuracy (verified by TA) and ≤5% hallucination rate.
  - Handle at least 50% of student questions without human escalation.
  - Collect sufficient data (usage logs, user surveys) to evaluate pilot success.
  - Stay within $5,000 budget.
- Non-goals:
  - Replace human teaching staff or office hours.
  - Support courses beyond the two pilot courses.
  - Answer questions outside approved materials (e.g., personal advice, unverified sources).
  - Achieve full automation of all student queries.

3. Stakeholder map
- Faculty sponsor (part-time): Provides course materials, approves content, oversees academic integrity.
- Student developers (2): Build and maintain the AI assistant (e.g., using a retrieval-augmented generation framework).
- Teaching assistant (TA): Validates answers, handles escalations, collects user feedback.
- Students (~300): End users of the assistant.
- Department head (informal): Approves pilot expansion based on results.
- IT support (consulted): Ensures data privacy and system security.

4. 12-week roadmap table with phases, tasks, owners, and deliverables

| Phase | Week | Tasks | Owner | Deliverables |
|-------|------|-------|-------|--------------|
| Foundation | 1-2 | Define approved materials (syllabi, lecture notes, readings) | Faculty sponsor | Approved material list |
| | | Set up development environment (e.g., cloud server, API keys) | Student developers | Development environment |
| | | Draft privacy and academic-integrity safeguards | Faculty sponsor + TA | Safeguard policy document |
| Development | 3-5 | Build retrieval system for approved materials | Student developers | Functional retrieval pipeline |
| | | Implement answer generation with hallucination guardrails | Student developers | AI assistant prototype |
| | | Integrate escalation mechanism (e.g., flag to TA) | Student developers | Escalation workflow |
| Testing | 6-8 | Internal testing with TA and faculty sponsor | TA + Faculty sponsor | Test results and bug reports |
| | | Refine answer accuracy and safety filters | Student developers | Updated assistant version |
| | | Prepare user onboarding materials (FAQ, instructions) | TA | User guide |
| Live deployment | 9-11 | Launch assistant to students in both courses | All team | Live assistant |
| | | Monitor usage, collect feedback, handle escalations | TA | Weekly usage reports |
| | | Conduct mid-pilot survey (week 10) | TA | Survey data |
| Evaluation | 12 | Analyze success metrics and budget | All team | Evaluation report |
| | | Present go/no-go recommendation to department head | Faculty sponsor | Decision gate presentation |

5. Resource and budget allocation
- Total budget: $5,000
  - Cloud hosting (e.g., AWS or similar): $1,500 (12 weeks)
  - API costs (e.g., for language model): $2,000 (estimated usage)
  - Student developer stipends (token compensation): $1,000
  - Miscellaneous (e.g., survey tools, printing): $500
- Assumption: Student developers and TA are volunteers or receive course credit; faculty sponsor is part-time salaried. No additional staff hired.

6. Risk register with mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| AI hallucinates answers | Medium | High | Use retrieval-augmented generation; restrict output to approved materials; implement confidence threshold; TA reviews flagged answers. |
| Privacy breach (e.g., student data exposed) | Low | High | Anonymize logs; do not store personal data; use encrypted connections; follow university data policy (assumed). |
| Low student adoption | Medium | Medium | Promote via course announcements; offer small incentive (e.g., extra credit for feedback). |
| Budget overrun | Low | Medium | Monitor API costs weekly; cap usage if needed; use free tier where possible. |
| Technical failure (e.g., downtime) | Medium | Medium | Build fallback (email TA); test thoroughly before launch. |
| Academic-integrity misuse (e.g., cheating) | Medium | High | Assistant only answers factual questions from materials; do not generate essays or solutions; include integrity warning. |

7. Success metrics and data collection plan
- Metrics:
  - Answer accuracy: % of answers verified correct by TA (target ≥80%).
  - Hallucination rate: % of answers containing unverified info (target ≤5%).
  - Escalation rate: % of questions forwarded to human staff (target ≤50%).
  - User satisfaction: average rating from survey (target ≥3.5/5).
  - Usage: number of unique users and questions per week.
  - Cost per question: total budget / total questions answered.
- Data collection:
  - Log all questions and answers (anonymized
